sources.name: GitHub REST API — repo metadata
sources.auth: PAT (Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}); unauth falls back but is gated — createGitHubAdapter throws when token missing unless STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK=true

rate_limit.tracked_via: x-ratelimit-remaining + x-ratelimit-reset headers, cached in GitHubApiAdapter.rateLimit, preemptively blocks requests until reset

retry_backoff_timeout.timeout_ms: none — relies on platform default; no AbortSignal.timeout

caching.note: grep for 'If-None-Match|ETag' returns zero hits; every call is a cold request

typical_latency_event_to_db_sec.how_estimated: cron cadence in vercel.json: hot=hourly (0 * * * *), warm=every 6h (0 */6 * * *), cold=daily (0 0 * * *). Worst-case GitHub event-time → snapshot row == next cron tick after event. Observed: vercel.json currently only contains redirects to Railway, so the cron cadence is whatever the Railway deploy runs.

failure_mode.404: fetchRepo returns null → IngestResult.ok=false with error='repo not found'; store is untouched (safe)
failure_mode.non_2xx_other: console.error, return null → silent skip; prior Repo/Score row retained, sparkline stays flat, no user-facing warning
failure_mode.network_error: up to 2 retries, then null → same as above
failure_mode.rate_limited: cron/ingest aborts batch and returns { ok:false, reason:'rate-limited' }; repos already processed are kept and recomputeAll runs
failure_mode.token_missing_production: createGitHubAdapter throws synchronously → HTTP 500 from cron endpoint (loud failure, correct)
failure_mode.mock_fallback_in_prod: NOT reachable — useMock=true requires STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK=true which env.example explicitly tells you to leave blank in prod. Verified src/lib/pipeline/ingestion/ingest.ts:240-249.

cost_model.current_300_repos: ~900 calls per full seed (18% of hourly budget). Steady-state cron: hot 50/hr + warm 20/6hr + cold 5/day ≈ 63 calls/hr peak = 1.3% of budget.
cost_model.x10_3000_repos: full seed = 9000 calls → needs ~1.8hr (breaks 1-hour budget). Steady-state hot tier alone capped at 50/hr by scheduler; actual refresh cadence drops to once per 60 hours for any given repo in hot. This is the scalability ceiling.
cost_model.x100_30000_repos: full seed = 90,000 calls → 18 hours. Single-token model breaks: need token pool, app-installation, or BigQuery/GHArchive backfill (neither wired in).

sources.name: GitHub REST API — stargazers backfill
sources.auth: PAT required (hard error without token — see stargazer-backfill.ts:70)

rate_limit.implication: mega-repos >40k stars cannot be backfilled via this endpoint — GitHub caps pagination at 400 pages × 100 = 40,000 users, oldest-first. See events-backfill.ts:3 comment block.

retry_backoff_timeout.retries: NONE — direct fetch, no exponential backoff wrapper

typical_latency_event_to_db_sec.estimate: driven by manual/cron invocation. A run writes 30 backdated daily snapshots computed from the full stargazer history the endpoint returns — so once run, day-granularity history is exact. Staleness after the run == time until the next backfill call (currently there is no scheduled /cron/backfill-top cron in vercel.json). Forward freshness relies on the ingest cron running hourly/daily.
typical_latency_event_to_db_sec.evidence: .data/snapshots.jsonl contains 30 daily rows each for 13 repos (chroma-core/chroma, slint-ui/slint, tanstack/router, tokio-rs/tokio, weaviate/weaviate, yarnpkg/berry, semgrep/semgrep, nomicfoundation/hardhat, + a few others). Remaining 296 repos have no daily snapshots.

failure_mode.probe_non_ok: throws (src/lib/pipeline/ingestion/stargazer-backfill.ts:145). Surfaced as HTTP 500 by /api/pipeline/rebuild per-repo catch
failure_mode.list_cap_exceeded: returns { skipped: 'exceeds_list_cap' }. Rebuild route (route.ts:166) falls through to backfillFromEvents as Plan B
failure_mode.rate_limit_tight: aborts current run, preserves already-written snapshots, next call resumes
failure_mode.result_leaves_sparklines_empty_for: all repos >40k stars that never had backfillFromEvents run

cost_model.current_300_repos: 50 pages × 300 repos = 15,000 calls if backfilling every repo at full depth — 3 hours of budget. In practice operator-invoked with small maxPages (default 8 in rebuild, 50 in backfill-top).
cost_model.x10: 150,000 calls = 30 hours of budget. Infeasible without token rotation.
cost_model.x100: not viable. Would need GH Archive on BigQuery (not wired in).

sources.name: GitHub REST API — Events API fallback for mega-repos
sources.auth: PAT required (token argument is non-optional; events-backfill.ts:56 throws if missing — 60/hr unauth would melt)
sources.rate_limit: shared 5000/hr budget. Max 3 pages = 300 events per repo per call. Budget floor: stops when x-ratelimit-remaining < 200.
called_from: invoked ONLY by src/app/api/pipeline/rebuild/route.ts:167 when stargazer-backfill returned skipped='exceeds_list_cap'
sources.trigger_condition: repo.stars > 40,000 AND stargazer-backfill probe sees Link rel='last' page>=400. In practice: repo has enough stars that the stargazer list cap is hit.
sources.what_it_fetches: recent 90 days of repo activity, up to 300 events. Filters to WatchEvent (star actions), buckets by YYYY-MM-DD, builds daily star-delta map.

reconstructs_real_history_vs_fake.verdict: PARTIALLY real — the day-bucketed WatchEvent counts are real GitHub events with real created_at. BUT Events API truncates at 300 events per request: for very active mega-repos (e.g. ollama, langchain, next.js) those 300 events cover only the last few DAYS, so the remaining days in the 30-day reconstruction land at delta=0 and the running-total walk just repeats today's star count backward (see events-backfill.ts:139-164).
reconstructs_real_history_vs_fake.concrete_failure: ollama repo row in .data/repos.jsonl has sparklineData=[0,0,0,...,0,169220] — only the final day populated. langchain sparkline is all zeros. These are exactly the mega-repos that would hit this fallback; whether it ran or not, the visible history is effectively fake-looking (flat line) for large swathes of days.
reconstructs_real_history_vs_fake.mentionCount24h: always 0 in synthesized snapshots; the backfill does not try to reconstruct social mentions

sources.failure_mode: non-ok response breaks out of the page loop; whatever was collected is still written. Zero-delta days silently render as flat line in sparkline UI — indistinguishable from a repo with no recent stars.

cost_model.current_300_repos: maxPages=3 × ~10 mega-repos = ~30 calls per rebuild. Cheap.
cost_model.x100: ~3000 calls. Fine, but still limited by 90d/300-event horizon.

sources.name: GitHub Search API — cross-repo issue mentions
sources.auth: optional PAT. Unauth: 10 req/min. With token: 30 req/min. Much lower than core API.
sources.rate_limit: separate search-API bucket (not 5000/hr). Limit headers are respected implicitly; on 403/429 a single global 'ghRateLimitLogged' flag suppresses repeat logs.
called_from: ONLY invoked from detail page & /api/repos/[owner]/[name] (src/app/api/repos/[owner]/[name]/route.ts:83, src/app/repo/[owner]/[name]/page.tsx:139)
sources.typical_latency_event_to_db_sec: N/A — results are never persisted. Every detail-page request re-fetches live. No mentionStore append path.
sources.failure_mode: returns [] on non-ok, logs once. UI shows 'no mentions' section. Invisible to user.

cost_model.current_300_repos: 1 call per detail page view. Per-repo on-demand only.
cost_model.x10: same — scales with page views, not repo count.
cost_model.x100: same. Concern is search-API budget (30/min authenticated) colliding with heavy UI traffic.

sources.name: Hacker News — Algolia search index
sources.rate_limit: no published ceiling for reasonable use. No retry-after handling.
sources.typical_latency_event_to_db_sec: never stored — re-fetched on every detail request. HN→UI lag ≈ Algolia indexing lag (seconds to minutes) + 1 fetch round-trip.
sources.failure_mode: non-ok → console.error + return []. UI hides HN section silently.
sources.cost_model: free, unbounded; concern is only timeouts at high concurrency.

sources.name: Reddit — public unauthenticated search.json
sources.auth: none, but REQUIRES descriptive User-Agent ('StarScreener/1.0 (by /u/starscreener)'). Default undici/node-fetch UA gets 403/429.
sources.rate_limit: Reddit's anti-scraping rules. ~60 req/min/IP for unauth public JSON in practice. No retry-after handling.
called_from: same invocation sites as HN adapter — detail-page only
sources.dedup_logic: title/selftext must contain fullName OR repoName OR (owner length>=4). Anti-false-positive. See social-adapters.ts:349-360.
sources.typical_latency_event_to_db_sec: never stored. Re-fetched per detail request.
sources.failure_mode: non-ok → [], UI hides. Reddit is known to serve 429/403 under bot suspicion, which would be completely invisible to end users.
sources.cost_model: free. Risk is IP bans at scale on a shared Railway/Vercel IP.

sources.name: Twitter/X via Nitter mirror RSS (no Twitter API)
sources.wire: scrape (RSS over HTTPS from community-run mirrors)
endpoints: probe: GET https://{mirror}/about (checks for Nitter generator meta tag)
sources.probe_logic: on module load (src/lib/pipeline/adapters/nitter-adapter.ts:138). First mirror that (a) returns 200 on /about AND (b) body matches /<meta name="generator" content="Nitter/i wins. Stored in module-level nitterHost; TWITTER_AVAILABLE flag exported so UI can hide the tab entirely when no mirror reachable.
sources.dedup_logic: tweet id extracted from status/{id} URL path (nitter-adapter.ts:266). Falls back to postedMs+author string when unparseable.
sources.rate_limit: hard-coded 1 request / 10s globally across the whole process (nitter-adapter.ts:41, 144). Serialized via lastFetchAt timestamp.
called_from: wired conditionally: src/app/api/repos/[owner]/[name]/route.ts:85 — only if TWITTER_AVAILABLE is true
sources.failure_mode: fail-soft. No mirror → TWITTER_AVAILABLE=false → UI simply omits Twitter tab (no error text per design). Any mirror error → [] + log. Canonical twitter.com URL is restored for clicks.
sources.single_point_of_failure: YES — all 4 hardcoded mirrors are operated by volunteer third parties. nitter.net has been intermittently captcha-blocked; the list in code has no automatic refresh, so a cascading mirror failure results in Twitter section silently disappearing with no alert.
sources.cost_model: free. Risk is mirror IP blocks at scale — the global 10s rate limit caps RPM to 6, so all users of a single deploy contend for one shared token-bucket.

sources.name: MockGitHubAdapter (dev-only fixture adapter)
sources.endpoints: none — synthesizes GitHubRepoRaw from a provided Repo[]
sources.reachability_in_prod: BLOCKED. Factory in src/lib/pipeline/ingestion/ingest.ts:236 throws unless both (a) caller passes useMock=true AND (b) STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK=='true' env var is set. cron/ingest hard-codes useMock=false (src/app/api/cron/ingest/route.ts:283) and cron/seed + pipeline/rebuild both call createGitHubAdapter({ useMock: false }).
sources.remaining_risks: src/app/api/pipeline/ingest/route.ts:76 accepts body.useMock from the request. An authenticated caller (CRON_SECRET holder) can request useMock=true, but the factory still refuses unless STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK=true env var is also set. Double-gate is intact.

mock_escape_hatches.name: STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK env var
mock_escape_hatches.defined_in: .env.example:45 (explicitly blank with a 'leave blank in production' comment)
mock_escape_hatches.notes: Verified: unless the env var is literally the string 'true' AND the caller opts in with useMock=true, the factory throws. cron paths pin useMock=false. Only /api/pipeline/ingest accepts useMock from the JSON body, but still passes through the same factory gate.

mock_escape_hatches.name: MockGitHubAdapter with empty fixture set
mock_escape_hatches.notes: Constructor accepts repos:Repo[]=[] default. If reached with no fixtures, every fetch returns null/0 — never synthesizes data it wasn't given. Defensive.

mock_escape_hatches.name: MockGitHubAdapter.getRateLimit always returns remaining=5000
mock_escape_hatches.notes: Consistent with the broader 'no silent mock in prod' contract. Only active under explicit test/local-dev opt-in.

latency_histogram.how_estimated: stargazer backfill uses real starred_at timestamps from /stargazers endpoint; day-granularity is exact on the DB side. Day-end boundary means worst-case 86400s lag between a star event and its snapshot bucket — but within a given day the count is correct.
same as chroma — stargazer backfill has written 30 daily rows with monotonic star counts (22008 → 22263). Real GitHub timestamps.
30 real daily rows present. Stargazer timestamps are authoritative.

only TWO snapshots in store, both from the same seed run 2 minutes apart (07:53:53Z and 07:55:45Z). Same star count in both. No 30-day stargazer backfill — this mega-repo exceeds the 400-page list cap. No Events API fallback ran either (would have produced 30 rows). sparklineData is all zeros. Actual GitHub→DB lag for new stars is therefore at MINIMUM the time between ingest cron runs (1-24h); star-delta signals are effectively dead for this repo.
one snapshot. sparklineData in repos.jsonl is [0,0,...,0,169220] — 29 zeros then today. Classic exceeds_list_cap with no events-api fallback successfully populating days. Delta calcs are wrong.
sparklineData is all zeros including today. starsDelta24h/7d/30d all read 0. No snapshot ever written for this repo.
all-zeros sparkline. movementStatus='declining' is scored off all-zero deltas, not real decline.

snapshots.jsonl contains only 13 unique repos. The remaining ~296 seeded repos have ZERO snapshots stored — they were seeded once, recomputeAll fired against empty deltas, and nothing has refreshed them since. The hot-tier cron (if actually running on Railway) would slowly rebuild history one point per hour going forward.

current_300_repos.backfill_calls_per_repo: 1-8 pages × 1 calls = 1-8 (at default maxPages=4 in rebuild)
current_300_repos.social_calls_per_detail_view: 3 (HN + Reddit + GH-search) + 1 nitter probe if TWITTER_AVAILABLE; all on-demand, 0 written to DB
current_300_repos.note: GH Archive / BigQuery is NOT wired in. 100% of ingestion is GitHub REST.

x10_3000_repos.refresh_cadence_degrades: hot cap is 50/hr (DEFAULT_POLICIES.hot), so hot-tier repos get refreshed at best every 60h instead of every 1h — scheduler becomes the bottleneck, not the API.

required_changes: GitHub App installation (5000/hr/installation × N installations) OR
GitHub Archive + BigQuery for historical backfill — would replace stargazer-backfill for mega-repos, cost ~$5-50/mo depending on query shape (NOT wired in)

failure_modes_summary: Four distinct silent-degradation paths: (1) GitHub REST non-2xx → null → prior row retained → UI sees stale data with no indicator. (2) Stargazer backfill hits the 40k-star list cap → Events API fallback runs but is capped at 300 events / 90 days, so for mega-repos the 30-day sparkline is reconstructed mostly as flat-line zeros and deltas compute to 0. UI renders 'declining' (see huggingface/transformers) or 'stable' for repos that in reality are adding thousands of stars per day. (3) Social adapters (HN/Reddit/Nitter/GH-search) return [] on any error — detail-page mentions section silently empties with no error text. (4) Nitter probe failure → TWITTER_AVAILABLE=false module-wide → Twitter tab vanishes until process restart; no self-healing. Mock fallback is correctly gated and cannot be hit in prod.
no_mock_claim_evidence: Verified at src/lib/pipeline/ingestion/ingest.ts:236-258 — createGitHubAdapter throws synchronously when GITHUB_TOKEN is missing AND useMock is not explicitly true, AND useMock=true further requires STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK=true. Every cron route (cron/ingest:283, cron/seed, cron/backfill-top, pipeline/rebuild) pins useMock=false. .env.example:41-45 explicitly documents STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK as 'leave blank in production'. ingestRepo sets result.source='github' vs 'mock' purely based on adapter.id, so downstream code can distinguish. No code path silently falls back to mock on failure — failures surface as repo:null results, HTTP 500s, or empty arrays. The stricter claim in README — 'if a value can't be verified against GitHub, HN, Reddit, or Nitter, it isn't shown' — is PARTIALLY true: mega-repo sparklines are reconstructed from partial Events API data and DO appear in the UI as flat-zero lines, which is arguably 'showing unverified values' even if the backing adapter is real.

readme_misrepresentations.claim: README line 19: '300-repo curated seed ... plus nightly stargazer-backfill for the top 20 to produce real historical sparklines'
readme_misrepresentations.reality: There is no 'nightly' stargazer backfill wired up in vercel.json — vercel.json currently contains only Railway redirects (no crons section). /api/cron/backfill-top exists as an endpoint but nothing schedules it. Of 309 seeded repos, only 13 have stargazer-reconstructed 30-day history in snapshots.jsonl. The remaining 296 have zero or one snapshot row.

readme_misrepresentations.claim: README line 12: 'All numbers come from live sources — strict no-mock rule ... if a value can't be verified against GitHub, HN, Reddit, or Nitter, it isn't shown'
readme_misrepresentations.reality: Sparkline values of zero for mega-repos are computed/shown despite not being verified — they're the Events API fallback's default when 300 events don't cover a day. starsDelta24h/7d/30d of zero are shown even when no snapshot history exists to prove the delta is actually zero. momentumScore is still rendered on the back of those zeros.
Endpoint is /api/stream (src/app/api/stream/route.ts), not /api/events/stream. Minor but it breaks copy-paste docs.

readme_misrepresentations.claim: README implies Vercel cron schedule (ARCHITECTURE.md referenced, and docs/INGESTION.md:146 shows crons block)
readme_misrepresentations.reality: vercel.json has been reduced to a pure redirect config (Railway proxy). The crons block documented in INGESTION.md:150-156 is no longer in vercel.json. Scheduling is happening somewhere else (Railway scheduled jobs? GitHub Actions?) — or not at all.

single_point_of_failure.name: single GITHUB_TOKEN
single_point_of_failure.why: Every ingest path (core API, stargazer backfill, Events API fallback, GitHub search in social) pulls from process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN. A revoked/expired token kills the entire pipeline — ingest returns 500, cron stops writing snapshots, and within 24 hours every sparkline grows stale with no user-facing warning. There is no token rotation, no multi-token pool, no app-installation alternate path.
